I don’t want Strategy to die and don’t think it has to in order for BTC to bounce back.

thread_tweets.text:
the issue with adding more debt via convertibles is their current convertibles are already so OTM that there is an extremely high likelihood that ~$6B of debt will also have to be paid off well before the end of 2028 (similar to the last $1.38B for $1.5B of debt move)...

so one way or another - you're now raising debt to pay off debt

vs. previously they were raising debt via convertibles to stack more BTC

paying high yield on a non-yield bearing asset for a company w/ no real significant revenue is a losing game for everyone at the bottom

thread_tweets.text:
I'm honestly embarrassed to say I  never took it seriously enough.

Got blinded by them pressing the buy button and how it fit my bias.

Wasn't forced to think critically until the recent string of broken promises.

Honestly believe at this point the best path forward is a flush where Strategy and other DATs are forced to deleverage sooner rather than later (e.g. if the market survives and bounces now, it just delays the crash and gives the opportunity for the problem and resulting footprint of the fallout to grow larger)
thread_tweets.full_text:
I'm honestly embarrassed to say I  never took it seriously enough.

Got blinded by them pressing the buy button and how it fit my bias.

Wasn't forced to think critically until the recent string of broken promises.

Honestly believe at this point the best path forward is a flush where Strategy and other DATs are forced to deleverage sooner rather than later (e.g. if the market survives and bounces now, it just delays the crash and gives the opportunity for the problem and resulting footprint of the fallout to grow larger)

thread_tweets.text:
Was a huge red-flag in hindsight, but I justified it as defensible enough to the BTC Yield narrative that I wrote it off.

I just think I was also so disillusioned by the growth in AI / tech / commodities, etc. over the last year it made me look for any way possible to justify my bullish bias in Bitcoin that it *had* to eventually catch up.

Embarrassing to say especially with how vocally and obnoxiously bullish I've been through the majority of the last 8 months of bleeding in the market.

Still incredibly bullish on the industry and think it recovers stronger from this mess, because despite the post 2020 btc maximalists on the timeline, Bitcoin != MSTR, and it's success isn't dependent on one arrogant deranged figurehead at the top.

tweets.full_text:
Great thread from one year ago that’s more relevant than ever today.

I'm honestly embarrassed to say I  never took it seriously enough.

Got blinded by them pressing the buy button and how it fit my bias.

Wasn't forced to think critically until the recent string of broken promises.

Honestly believe at this point the best path forward is a flush where Strategy and other DATs are forced to deleverage sooner rather than later (e.g. if the market survives and bounces now, it just delays the crash and gives the opportunity for the problem and resulting footprint of the fallout to grow larger)

Was a huge red-flag in hindsight, but I justified it as defensible enough to the BTC Yield narrative that I wrote it off.

I just think I was also so disillusioned by the growth in AI / tech / commodities, etc. over the last year it made me look for any way possible to justify my bullish bias in Bitcoin that it *had* to eventually catch up.

Embarrassing to say especially with how vocally and obnoxiously bullish I've been through the majority of the last 8 months of bleeding in the market.

Still incredibly bullish on the industry and think it recovers stronger from this mess, because despite the post 2020 btc maximalists on the timeline, Bitcoin != MSTR, and it's success isn't dependent on one arrogant deranged figurehead at the top.

@lowstrife Appreciate it man

+1 to you for catching on way sooner

@soulshakedown33 @lowstrife haha happy to chat about anything else!

still down to talk through culture, pros and cons of various L1s, sports, whatever

@0xCastellanos @lowstrife 🤝

Appreciate it. Feels refreshing.

Still want more than anything else for this industry to succeed, and I believe it will.

tweets.text: War is inflationary. Not because it sends oil prices higher, but because governments pay for war by creating inflation. Instead of raising taxes, they run larger deficits, which central banks then monetize. War also diverts resources away from the production of consumer goods.

tweets.text: So far, over the first five months of 2026, the CPI is up 2.52%. If this pace continues for all of 2026, the CPI will rise 6.2%, the most since 2022. But I think the second half of the year will see a much bigger rise, sending the full year above 7%, the highest rate since 1981.

tweets.text:
intensity is a learned skill, as much as anything else is. The weird current trend that mixes super low volume with 'science' based lifting that negates heavy compounds imo is a pretty great way for beginners to spin their wheels by not training with enough stimulus to drive growth via either volume, intensity or major body movement patterns (which probs falls slightly under intensity in and of itself)

I like all these approaches in isolation, or with nuance, but no substitute for hard work and that's what you should learn early on in your lifting career (not to mention higher volume gives you more reps under the bar to get proficient at movements over time)
tweets.full_text:

tweets.text:
My late teens early 20’s were wild times

Thankful to have survived it
tweets.full_text:
My late teens early 20’s were wild times

Thankful to have survived it

tweets.text: When I was 18 I got sucker punched and jumped outside a store by a group of black kids for no reason just wrong place wrong time. They used to call it the knock out game but thankfully I didn’t get knocked out. Was able to fight my way out of the crowd and run while watching my “friends” who brought me to the store drive off in fear. My jaw is still fucked up from that night but since then I’ve never thought to myself I wish I had a gun or a knife that night so I coulda killed somebody
